task: I need to text a coworker about covering the last hour of my shift because something came up. What’s a simple way to word it so the handoff is clear and nothing gets missed?

turns.agent_response:
**“Something came up — can you cover my last hour?”**

If you want it a touch clearer:
**“Something came up — can you cover my last hour and keep an eye on my tables?”**

user_simulator.text:
Close, but a little thin. I’d want it to say what to watch so nothing gets dropped.

Try:
"Something came up and I need to leave after this hour. Can you cover my last hour and keep an eye on my tables, tickets, and any checks still open? I’ll make sure everything is as caught up as I can before I go."

If you want, I can make it more casual or more firm.

turns.agent_response:
**“Something came up and I need to leave after this hour. Can you cover my last hour and keep an eye on my tables, tickets, and any open checks? I’ll get everything as caught up as I can before I go.”**

If you want, I can make it **shorter** or **more casual**.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Clear enough. I’d probably send it like that.
